Global (All).title: Iran protests: Violence and unrest reported in several cities
Global (All).summary: Demonstrations against the government have turned violent in several cities across Iran

Global (All).title: North Korea conducts missile test: South Korea
Global (All).summary: North Korea has fired a ballistic missile, South Korea's military says

Global (All).title: Israel-Gaza conflict: Gaza militants, Israel exchange fire
Global (All).summary: Rocket fire and artillery exchanges have been reported in the Israel-Gaza border region

United States.title: Supreme Court Leak Fuels Abortion Debate
United States.summary: The leaked draft of a Supreme Court opinion that would overturn Roe v. Wade has sparked intense debate in the US, with many fearing for the future of abortion rights.

United States.title: Twitter Hacker Exposes Private Data
United States.summary: A hacker gained access to Twitter's internal tools and exposed private data of several high-profile users, highlighting concerns about the platform's security and vulnerability.

United States.title: Protests Erupt in Response to Police Killing
United States.summary: Protesters took to the streets in multiple cities across the US after a police shooting left a young black man dead, highlighting ongoing tensions between law enforcement and communities of color.

South Korea.title: Government Crackdown on Labor Union Demands

Japan.title: Japan's PM Fumio Kishida Faces Pressure Over Handling of COVID-19
Japan.summary: The pressure mounts on Japan's PM to resign over his handling of COVID-19, following a surge in cases and the resignation of several senior ministers.

Japan.title: China, Japan to Hold Security Talks Amid Rising Tensions
Japan.summary: China and Japan have agreed to hold high-level security talks amid rising tensions over the South China Sea and North Korea's nuclear program.

Japan.title: Japan's Economy Suffers Slowest Quarterly Growth in Over 2 Years, Fueling Fears of Recession
Japan.summary: Japan's economy has suffered its slowest quarterly growth in over 2 years, fueling fears of recession and putting pressure on the government to implement more economic stimulus measures.
